payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Source elements present in the ledger.

original_descriptions: Weekly pocket money task completion and tracking cycle
Enable children to claim and track completed pocket money tasks
Provide children visibility into task inventory availability and completion status

payload.name: Household Participation
payload.description: Defines the fundamental relationships and roles of individuals participating in the household economy, establishing who has standing to engage in compensated activities and governance oversight.

payload.name: Compensated Work Accountability
payload.description: Establishes enterprise accountability for defining, assigning, executing, and tracking compensated work activities with associated monetary obligations, ensuring transparent record of effort and entitlement.

payload.name: Earnings Transparency and Stewardship
payload.description: Governs the enterprise obligation to provide clear, timely visibility into earned compensation and performance outcomes, enabling informed governance decisions regarding payment distribution and fairness.

payload.name: Historical Performance Stewardship
payload.description: Represents the enterprise commitment to maintain longitudinal records of work activity and performance, supporting accountability verification, trend analysis, and retrospective governance review.

payload.name: Task Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for defining, maintaining, and governing the lifecycle of monetarily-valued tasks that constitute the foundation of the reward system, including their availability patterns and temporal boundaries.

payload.name: Completion Accountability
payload.description: Business responsibility for recognizing and recording when children fulfill task obligations, establishing the basis for earned value and triggering downstream settlement obligations.

payload.name: Earnings Settlement
payload.description: Business responsibility for translating completed task activity into recognized monetary obligations owed to children, including periodic aggregation and preparation for parent fulfillment.

payload.name: Performance Oversight
payload.description: Business responsibility for providing parent visibility into child-task-earnings relationships to enable informed payment decisions and stewardship of the reward system.

payload.name: Historical Record Retention
payload.description: Business responsibility for preserving task and earnings information across time periods to support auditability, trend analysis, and long-term accountability independent of active cycles.

payload.name: Participation Entitlement
payload.description: Business responsibility for governing what task and earnings information children may access based on temporal scope and completion status, ensuring appropriate boundaries on child visibility.

payload.name: Earnings Derivation and Aggregation Logic
payload.description: Logical transformation model for deriving weekly earned amounts for child entities and aggregating task completion and earnings data for parent visibility without specifying computational algorithms.

payload.name: Task Lifecycle State Model
payload.description: Logical model governing task entity state transitions, completion event relationships, and the state dependencies that determine when tasks may progress through their operational lifecycle.

payload.name: Task Availability and Persistence Model
payload.description: Logical model defining temporal availability patterns for task entities and the integrity constraints governing historical task data persistence across operational periods.

payload.name: OS Platform Compatibility Control
payload.description: Physical layer governance of operating system version requirements that constrain the selection and configuration of deployment targets across the system infrastructure.

payload.name: Multi-Platform Deployment Infrastructure
payload.description: Detailed deployment specifications for mobile and desktop platform nodes, including iOS, Android, and Windows runtime environments with precise version requirements and configuration parameters.

payload.name: Child Actor Interface Specification
payload.description: Detailed user interface contract specifications for child-facing interactions, including task claiming component definitions, task viewing layouts, and weekly earnings access interface controls with precise interaction behaviors.

payload.name: Parent Actor Interface Specification
payload.description: Detailed user interface contract specifications for parent-facing interactions, including task configuration component definitions, distribution report access layouts, and administrative control interface behaviors.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Domain elements present in the ledger.

payload.title: High Level Description
payload.description: Section: High Level Description

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Segment elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Expresses the fundamental purpose of enabling children to claim and track completed pocket money tasks weekly.
Articulates parental motivation to define compensated task assignments with associated monetary values.
Captures the purpose of providing children visibility into available and completed task inventory.
Identifies the motivational goal of enabling children to view their weekly earnings summary.
Establishes parental need to access child performance reporting for accurate compensation distribution purposes.
Indicates stakeholder requirement for longitudinal task history access supporting accountability or trend analysis.
Section header indicating the commencement of conceptual system description.
Establishes the core business concept of children claiming completed tasks for monetary reward tracking.
Defines the parent capability to specify task entities with associated monetary value attributes.
Specifies the relationship between completed task entities and child entities through linkage.
Defines child actor visibility requirements for both available and completed task entities within temporal scope.
Establishes weekly aggregation capability for earned monetary totals visible to child actors.
Defines parent reporting capability displaying child-task-earnings relationships to support payment fulfillment.
Specifies the cyclical business rule for task availability reset at weekly temporal boundaries.
Mandates retention and accessibility of historical task data entities across temporal periods.
Logical association rule defining how task completion events are linked to child entities.
Data visibility requirements specifying logical access patterns for child users to task collections.
Temporal aggregation logic defining weekly computation and presentation of earned amounts to children.
Logical reporting interface providing parents aggregated view of child earnings and task completion data.
Periodic state reset logic defining weekly cycle behavior for task availability restoration.
Data retention requirement mandating logical persistence and accessibility of historical task records.
Platform compatibility constraint specifying concrete operating system versions for deployment.
Identifies children as operational actors who execute task claiming activities throughout the week on a temporal basis.
Identifies parents as operational actors responsible for task configuration and monetary value assignment activities.
Defines operational visibility requirements for child actors viewing available and completed tasks within weekly cycles.
Establishes weekly operational schedule boundary when child actors can access earned amount totals.
Defines parent operational role in accessing reports to execute pocket money distribution activities.
Specifies weekly operational cycle timing when task availability resets for claiming operations.
Defines deployment platform locations specifying where the system operates across iOS, Android, and Windows environments.
